turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having a persistent cough that started dry and now has greenish-yellow sputum, and it's been going on for 5 days. I'm also experiencing difficulty breathing and wheezing, especially when I try to talk or exert myself.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: My persistent cough is really getting to me - it started out dry but now it's productive with yellowish-green sputum, and it just won't go away. I'm also having trouble breathing and wheezing, which makes everyday activities a challenge.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about 5 days ago with just a dry cough, but over the past few days, it's become more productive with yellowish-green sputum. The cough is really bothering me and making it hard to breathe, especially in the evenings when my low-grade fever seems to get worse.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: The coughing episodes are constant now, I'd say at least 10-15 times a day, and they're really disrupting my daily life. I've even had to miss work because of it - I've been out for 3 days already due to fatigue and malaise from trying to manage this persistent cough.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's an 8 or 9, honestly. It's getting to the point where I'm really struggling with everyday activities and just want some relief from this constant coughing and difficulty breathing.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: I've also been experiencing a low-grade fever that's around 38.2°C (100.8°F) on most evenings, and I wake up drenched in sweat every night, which is really disrupting my sleep. Additionally, I've noticed that my fatigue and malaise are making it hard for me to concentrate at work or even do simple tasks during the day.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: Yes, the coughing gets significantly worse when I try to talk or exert myself, like when I'm teaching my students in class. It's really uncomfortable and makes me feel like I'm going to lose my breath, which is terrifying.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: Yes, I've been experiencing night sweats, soaking through my pajamas every night for 4 nights now, and I feel really fatigued and dizzy when I try to stand up or exert myself. The combination of these symptoms is really worrying me and making it hard for me to get out of bed in the morning.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: Yes, I've been experiencing right-sided chest pain that's sharp and worsens with deep breathing and coughing. It's a bit uncomfortable, but it's not constant, only when I'm experiencing these episodes of coughing and difficulty breathing.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any swelling in my legs or feet. But to be honest, I'm really worried about this whole situation - the constant coughing, difficulty breathing, fever, and night sweats are all just so overwhelming and scaring me a bit.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I've been taking over-the-counter dextromethorphan (cough suppressant) for the past week with minimal relief from my symptoms. I also take cetirizine 10mg as needed during allergy season, but it's not helping much with this persistent cough and difficulty breathing.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any pre-existing medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes. But I do have seasonal allergies (allergic rhinitis) since my teenage years, and I take cetirizine 10mg as needed to manage them.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. But honestly, with all these symptoms I'm experiencing, I'm more concerned about getting a diagnosis for what's going on with my cough and breathing difficulties than checking my cholesterol levels right now.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I've never smoked, but I do have social alcohol use, typically 1-2 glasses of wine on weekends.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: To be honest, my diet has been pretty bad lately. With this persistent cough and difficulty breathing, I haven't been feeling like cooking or eating much, so I've just been grabbing whatever's convenient, which isn't really ideal. As for exercise, I used to be pretty active, but with all these symptoms, I've had to scale back a lot - even just walking around my apartment can leave me winded and exhausted.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. My mother actually has asthma, which is something that's always been a concern for our family given her condition. But to my knowledge, there's no history of heart disease or heart attacks in our family.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My mother actually has asthma, which is something that we've always been aware of and monitored closely. As for any other serious medical conditions, I'm not really sure... there's a bit of a sensitive topic with my maternal grandmother passing away from lung cancer, but I'd rather not go into details about that right now.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: It's been around a year ago when I had my last physical exam. Everything checked out normal back then, but I'm really hoping we can figure out what's going on with this persistent cough and difficulty breathing now.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: Yes, I'm allergic to penicillin - it causes a rash.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: I've been working with young children daily at my job as an elementary school teacher, and several students in my class have had upper respiratory infections recently. I also live in Portland, Oregon, where the air quality can be a bit poor sometimes, but I'm not sure if that's relevant to my symptoms.
